mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in Industrial Production Index reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 20-day SPX returns is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income and consumption demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger fires during the evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated inflation and a near-zero federal funds rate creates deeply negative real policy rates, which force investors out of cash and into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 6% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in housing starts predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns specifically during post-recession recovery phases when construction multipliers are largest and credit spreads are compressing.
failure_condition: During post-recession recovery periods, HOUST growth fails to positively predict 60-day SPX returns with a t-statistic above 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in real retail sales signals weakening consumer demand and immediate downward pressure on corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when RSAFS declines.

mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate acts as a drag on equity valuations; higher levels increase the cost of capital and reduce discounted present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2%, positive 60-day SPX returns occur more frequently than negative returns over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial Production growth combined with falling unemployment confirms a non-inflationary supply expansion that supports profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint z-score of INDPRO and UNRATE fails to produce a Rank IC above 0.03 against 20-day SPX returns in any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation erodes real household purchasing power and compresses consumer-facing profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 4%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered observations.
